history.notes: TikTok cost-per-wear spreadsheet trend + underconsumption core (46M+ views) signal demand for a focused clothing cost tracker with zero dedicated competition.
Advancing to research — spawning Validator subagent

KILL: MRR math fails — WearWise is a free dedicated CPW app, Stylebook offers full wardrobe mgmt for $5.99 one-time; charging $8/mo for a subset of existing features yields <$500 MRR. 6 competitors already track cost-per-wear.

killReasons: Monetization potential LOW: WearWise (dedicated CPW app) is free with no subscription. Stylebook offers full wardrobe management including CPW for $5.99 one-time. Charging $1.99/week ($8/mo) for a single-feature app competing against free/cheap alternatives will not convert. MRR math: ~500-1000 downloads/mo * 4% conversion * $8/mo = $160-$320/mo.

Scout's 'zero dedicated competition' claim was incorrect: WearWise: Cost Per Wear (App Store ID 6759916371) is a dedicated CPW tracker. Additionally, Stylebook (7,547 reviews), Whering (8,046 reviews), Clueless, Acloset, and Indyx all include CPW tracking as a feature.

demandSignals: TikTok #underconsumptioncore: 46M+ views with creators sharing CPW spreadsheets and tutorials
Manual spreadsheet workaround: users tracking CPW in spreadsheets indicates unmet app demand
Reddit/forum communities: r/frugalmalefashion, YouLookFab Forum, Styleforum all have active CPW discussions
Blog/Substack content: multiple dedicated articles on tracking wardrobe cost-per-wear (doesthislookweird.substack.com, projectcece.com, thewelldressedlife.com)
marketSize.notes: Genuine demand exists — 4 independent signals confirmed. The underconsumption core movement is mainstream (Wikipedia article, CNBC coverage, WWD coverage). However, demand is already being served by existing apps.

topApps.name: WearWise: Cost Per Wear
topApps.notes: DEDICATED cost-per-wear tracker — direct competitor. New app, limited reviews visible.

topApps.name: Stylebook
topApps.notes: Gold standard wardrobe app since 2010. Full CPW tracking, wardrobe analytics, outfit planning.

topApps.name: Whering
topApps.notes: 7M+ users. Dragon's Den featured. CPW tracking included. Social features.

topApps.name: Clueless
topApps.notes: AI-powered outfit planning with CPW tracking. Newer entrant.

topApps.name: Acloset
topApps.notes: Basic wardrobe analytics including CPW and wear frequency.

topApps.name: Indyx
topApps.notes: Free wardrobe app with CPW tracking and stylist guidance.

competitors.identifiedGap: No clear gap. WearWise already occupies the 'simple dedicated CPW tracker' niche. Stylebook and Whering dominate the feature-rich space with thousands of reviews. A new entrant would need significant differentiation beyond what the concept proposes.
competitors.notes: 6 competitors found, under the 10-app saturation threshold. However, the niche of 'dedicated CPW tracker' is already taken by WearWise, and the broader 'wardrobe app with CPW' space has entrenched players with 7K-8K reviews.

monetization.mrrMath: ASO keyword popularity 28 (low search volume) → est. 500-1000 organic downloads/mo. At 4% conversion to $1.99/week ($8/mo effective): 500 * 0.04 * $8 = $160/mo optimistic. Even at 1000 downloads: $320/mo. Both well below $500 threshold. Users have free alternatives (WearWise) and a $5.99 one-time option (Stylebook) that includes far more features.
monetization.notes: The pricing environment makes subscription monetization extremely difficult. WearWise is free. Stylebook is $5.99 once and includes CPW plus full wardrobe management. Asking $8/mo for less functionality than a $6 one-time app is a losing proposition.

technicalFeasibility.notes: Technically trivial — SwiftUI, local CoreData/SwiftData storage, camera for item photos, simple arithmetic. No APIs needed, no backend required. This dimension is not the issue.

appStoreRisk.notes: Standard utility app. No health claims, financial advice, or guideline gray areas. Camera permission for item photos is routine.
